output.final_deliverable:
Chain (as far as the provided evidence supports):
1) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
2) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents/Portals

Notes: I cannot produce a 4‑URL chain from the given merged results because subsequent visit actions failed and the Main Page visit's links_sample did not include additional en.wikipedia.org pages to follow. The search result included the Contents/Portals page, but there is no visited-page evidence showing that Main Page actually links to it in the provided visit output.
output.action_summary:
What I have from the merged results:
- A visited Main Page record: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page (visit succeeded; links_sample shown included non-en.wikipedia pages such as https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Wikidata:Main_Page, https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/Main_Page, https://en.wikiversity.org/wiki/Wikiversity:Main_Page).
- A search result that lists https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents/Portals.

Missing evidence required to complete the task:
- A visit result showing the Main Page's full link list that includes https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents/Portals (to verify the adjacency Main_Page -> Contents/Portals).
- Subsequent successful visit outputs for pages linked from Contents/Portals (or other Main Page links) so I can continue the chain to at least 4 en.wikipedia.org URLs and produce a verified adjacency pair.

URLs cited from the merged results:
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents/Portals

results.title: Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
results.description: Content portals – A unique way to navigate the encyclopedia. Wikipedia is written by volunteer editors and hosted by the Wikimedia Foundation, a non-profit organization that also hosts a range of other volunteer projects:

results.title: Wikipedia:Contents/Portals - Wikipedia
results.description: Portals complement main topics in Wikipedia, and expand upon topics by introducing the reader to key articles, images, and categories that further describe the subject and its related topics. Portals also assist in helping editors to find related projects and things they can do to improve ...

results.title: Wikipedia:Main Page history - Wikipedia
results.description: The main page was redesigned to include a featured article for a given day in a prominent position starting in February 2004, an arrangement that persists to this day. The Did You Know section for newly-added content also dates to February 2004, with the Picture of the Day dating to May 2004. The modern design for Wikipedia's Main Page was largely completed in 2006. A community effort that began in December 2004 to redesign the Main Page and key pages linked from it was implemented in March 2006 after several months of discussion and input from the community organized by the Usability WikiProject.

results.title: Wikipedia:Portal - Wikipedia
results.description: Portals are created for encyclopedic topics only and not for article maintenance categories. Portals are largely inter-accessible, with users able to navigate from one portal to another. Universal features, such as the portals browsebar (which links to top-level portals), and the portals template (which links to Wikipedia:Contents/Portals), allow for convenient browsing.

results.title: Wikipedia:About the Main Page - Wikipedia
results.description: Besides by the link in the left ... will take one to the main page for that language of Wikipedia.) at the top-left of the Wikipedia logo, and a link to it is displayed on Wikipedia's Bad title pages....

results.title: Help:Navigation - Wikipedia
results.description: There's a browsing bar at the top ... are illustrated article summaries like on the Main Page. "Featured content", on the side bar, is the way to the best articles, pictures, lists and portals in the encyclopedia....

results.title: Wikipedia:Contents/Portals/(new) - Wikipedia
results.description: Portals complement main topics in Wikipedia, and expound upon topics by introducing the reader to key articles, images, and categories that further describe the subject and its related topics. Portals also assist in helping editors to find related projects and things they can do to improve ...

results.title: Wikipedia:WikiProject Portals - Wikipedia
results.description: Portal:Mathematics, for instance, provides a selection of reading samples including more obscure and trivial topics, such as DYKs, chosen and structured to provoke interest, beside a guide to related on-wiki projects, all underscored by a comprehensive, collapsible link tree for users drawn deeper into the subject. Portals begin as a basic template. But the design of the portal progresses and evolves to the stage where static content display transforms to dynamic user interface. With interactive components such as slideshows and rotating featured content (emulating a main page), portals aspire to be a go-to area for seeking knowledge in a particular categorical subject.

results.title: Wikipedia:FAQ/Main Page - Wikipedia
results.description: There are externally hosted feeds of the featured article and the picture of the day, along with some other Wikipedia pages; please see Wikipedia:Syndication. It has been argued that the Main Page should be at Wikipedia:Main Page, Portal:Main Page, or some other location.

results.title: Wikipedia:Portal/Guidelines - Wikipedia
results.description: Portals should not be a vehicle for advocacy or advertisement, should not have too many red links, should not be redundant to another portal, should not cover too narrow a scope, and should adhere to Wikipedia's policy on neutrality. For speedy deletion of portals, see Wikipedia:CSD#Portals and for deletion by discussion see WP:MFD. It is common practice not to include references in portals. As on the Main Page, readers should be able to verify the portal content by following a prominent link to a relevant article, and checking the references there.

results.title: Wikipedia:Main Page/1 - Wikipedia
results.description: Content portals – A unique way to navigate the encyclopedia. Wikipedia is written by volunteer editors and hosted by the Wikimedia Foundation, a non-profit organization that also hosts a range of other volunteer projects:

results.title: Wikipedia talk:Contents/Portals/Archive 4 - Wikipedia
results.description: The Main Page Featured Portal drive was successfully completed. This was a collaborative initiative created to get all portals linked from the top-right of the Main Page to Featured Portal quality status.

results.title: Wikipedia:Main Page/Tomorrow - Wikipedia
results.description: Content portals – A unique way to navigate the encyclopedia. Wikipedia is written by volunteer editors and hosted by the Wikimedia Foundation, a non-profit organization that also hosts a range of other volunteer projects:

results.title: Category:Portals - Wikipedia
results.description: A portal is a focus page which highlights a particular subject to complement the main article on that subject. Portals feature articles covering the portal's theme, and generally provide lists of related articles, and thus provide an alternative means to browse on Wikipedia.

results.title: History of Wikipedia - Wikipedia
results.description: However, the concept was pioneered on the German Wikipedia, where Portal:Recht (law studies) was set up in October 2003. On 16 July 2005, the English Wikipedia began the practice of including the day's "featured pictures" on the Main Page. On 19 March 2006, following a vote, the Main Page of the English-language Wikipedia featured its first redesign in nearly two years. On 13 May 2010, the site released a new interface. New features included an updated logo, new navigation tools, and a link wizard.

results.title: Wikipedia:Community portal - Wikipedia
results.description: The feature prompts newcomers to add a citation before publishing new content, helping reduce common citation-related reverts and improve verifiability. In A/B testing, the impact was substantial: newcomers shown Reference Check were approximately 2.2 times more likely to include a reference on desktop and about 17.5 times more likely on mobile web. [1] ... The InterwikiSorting extension, which allowed for the sorting of interwiki links, has been undeployed from Wikipedia.

results.title: Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Usability/Main Page/Final archive - Wikipedia
results.description: We didn't want to force people ... and the portal link assortment as a package deal, so we left the latter unaltered for the time being. —David Levy 07:19, 1 March 2006 (UTC) Support. The Tom 07:15, 1 March 2006 (UTC) ... Support. I like boxes. GreenReaper 07:28, 1 March 2006 (UTC) Support.The addition featured photo is ...

results.title: Help:Getting started - Wikipedia
results.description: The contributing to Wikipedia page provides information, links, videos and other resources on the basics needed to edit Wikipedia. The five pillars is a popular summary of the most pertinent Wikipedia principles. The community portal is a central location to find collaborations, tasks, and news about Wikipedia. There are volunteers available to answer your questions at the Teahouse. You can always go to your mentor with questions too. Introduction: our main tutorial to the core principles of how to edit contained in thirteen short modules (as listed below).

details.intent: Load the Main Page and extract inline links to be used as the next nodes in a chain of Wikipedia pages.
target_facts: Collect up to 30 distinct in-page links from the Main Page (format: Link text [link: <exact URL>]).
For each link: capture link text, exact URL (from _links_inline), and the HTML context (the paragraph or list item containing the link).
details.extraction_plan: Return full page content and ensure the tool-provided '_links_inline' field is captured. From that field, select up to 3 candidate URLs to continue the chain. Provide the exact URL strings so subsequent visit nodes can consume them.
measurable_outputs: links_count: integer (number of links extracted, target >= 3)
links_sample: array of objects {text, url, context_snippet} (up to 3 entries)
details.rationale: This is the mandated starting page. Extracting inline links directly from the Main Page produces verifiable URLs (from the page content) that will be used to build the required chain. Doing this first removes uncertainty about which links are actually present on the Main Page instead of guessing.
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
details.original_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
details.goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

700acd0b-8987-44f7-98c4-25fd12ec3a1a.title: Search the Main Page results for candidate links to follow (produce URLs)

details.intent: Supplement the direct visit extraction by searching for pages that are commonly linked from the Main Page to ensure we have concrete URL candidates for the next visits.
target_facts: Produce at least 3 distinct Wikipedia URLs that are likely to be present as links on the Main Page (URLs must be exact, e.g., https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents).
Return snippet evidence for each URL showing it was referenced in context (so we can cross-check against the Main Page visit).
verification_steps: Compare the URLs returned here with the _links_inline output from the Main Page visit. Only use URLs that appear in both sources (ensures the URL actually is present on the Main Page).
If fewer than 3 overlapping URLs are found, expand search queries to include other common Main Page link terms (e.g., 'Current events', 'Featured content', 'On this day').
measurable_outputs: overlap_count_with_main_visit: integer (how many of these URLs were present in the visit output)
details.rationale: Because subsequent visit nodes must consume real URLs that come from the previous page, this search produces candidate URLs (snippets with exact URLs) and provides cross-checkable evidence (snippets) so we can pick valid next pages without guessing.
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
details.original_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
details.goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

108a03d6-85d0-4108-beeb-d2da8d41105d.title: Visit next page: follow first verified link from Main Page (consume URL produced by visit/search)

details.url: TO_BE_FILLED_FROM_PARENT_RESULTS__(select the first URL that appears in both the Main Page visit _links_inline and the search results)
details.intent: Open the first verified URL that was found on the Main Page and extract its inline links so we can continue chaining.
target_facts: Confirm that this page was linked from the Main Page (capture the exact anchor text and context where Main Page links to this URL, if available).
Extract up to 20 inline links from this page (format: Link text [link: <exact URL>]).
details.extraction_plan: Use the exact URL produced by the earlier steps. Capture the page's full content and the tool '_links_inline' field. From those links, choose the next URL for node 4 (must be one of this page's inline links).
measurable_outputs: this_page_links_sample: array of {text, url, context_snippet} (up to 3 entries)
details.rationale: To maintain the rule that each next URL comes from links on the previous visited page, this visit consumes a URL verified from the Main Page. Extracting this page's links provides the next set of verifiable candidates for continuing the chain.
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
details.original_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

c36416c9-1bf0-4c80-b126-2c5012c6ea62.title: Visit next page: follow first verified link from previous page (consume URL produced by previous visit)

details.url: TO_BE_FILLED_FROM_PARENT_RESULTS__(select the first URL from the previous page's '_links_inline' results)
details.intent: Open the second chained page (third page in the overall chain) and extract inline links so we can produce the final page of the chain.
target_facts: Confirm that this page was linked from the immediately prior page by capturing the exact anchor and context from the prior page's '_links_inline'.
Extract up to 20 inline links from this page to be used for the next step.
details.extraction_plan: Use the exact URL chosen from the prior visit's _links_inline. Capture '_links_inline' content and pick one link that will be visited next. Prepare data for adjacency verification between this page and the next page.
measurable_outputs: chosen_next_url: string (exact URL intended for next visit)
details.rationale: This step continues the chain and provides the penultimate set of inline links; it is required so that the final page is verifiably linked from the previous page, enabling the required adjacency verification.
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
details.original_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

1b3ab1a8-2d0e-4a2e-8544-d34e6419eae9.title: Visit final page: follow a verified link from the immediately prior page (consume URL produced by previous visit)

details.url: TO_BE_FILLED_FROM_PARENT_RESULTS__(the chosen_next_url extracted from the prior visit's _links_inline)
details.intent: Visit the final page in the chain (making at least 4 total Wikipedia URLs including the Main Page). Extract evidence proving the adjacency from the prior page to this page.
target_facts: Capture the full page content and HTML context where the prior page links to this URL (we will later show the adjacency pair with exact evidence).
Extract minimal metadata (title, first paragraph) for reporting the chain.
details.extraction_plan: Use the exact URL selected from the previous visit's '_links_inline'. Return the page content and ensure that the prior page's link to this URL can be referenced (the prior page's visit output should include the anchor with exact URL).
measurable_outputs: adjacency_evidence_pointer: reference to prior_visit._links_inline entry that contains this final_page_url
details.rationale: This completes the required chain length (>=4). Collecting the final page and the exact pointer to the prior page's _links_inline entry allows us to produce the requested verified adjacency pair showing URL[i] links to URL[i+1].
